name: Komodo Dragon

visual_attributes.morphology: Large, robust lizard with a long tail and powerful limbs.
distinctive_features: Serrated teeth up to 2.5 cm in length
visual_attributes.texture: Scaly and rugged with armored scales
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Males up to 3 m (10 ft) long and 150 kg (330 lb)
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Tail as long as the body, robust limbs, elongated head

domain_specific_visual.OSTEODERMS: Four types: rosette, platy, dendritic, and vermiform
domain_specific_visual.PARIETAL_EYE: Light-sensing organ on top of the head

relationships.target: Indonesian islands of Komodo, Rinca, Flores, Gili Dasami, and Gili Motang

relationships.description: Shares a common ancestor with the Australian lace monitor

contextual_attributes.historical_period: First recorded by Western scientists in 1910
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Considered spiritually significant by the Komodo people

contrastive_constraints: NOT found outside its native Indonesian islands
